summary:
Introducing cereals before six months could increase the risk of food allergies in infants because their immune systems are not yet mature enough to handle potential allergens. This is supported by current recommendations delaying solid food intake until at least 6 months to minimize such risks.

Final check: Does it stay within core medical facts? Yes – specifically addressing immunological vulnerability leading to hypersensitivity reactions when exposing immature infants to common allergens via premature cereal consumption.
